Green and oolong tea kombuchas emerged as the most biologically active, while fermentation transformed each tea into a distinctly different beverage.

articles.title: Critics skeptical of Canadian study that found only 3% of trans-identifying youth detransition
articles.description: A study of 445 teens treated at Canadian gender clinics found that few detransitioned but critics say the follow-up time was too short

articles.description: SETI scientists searched the interstellar object 3I/ATLAS for radio signals that could indicate extraterrestrial technology but found nothing beyond human-made interference. articles.description: The alleged suspect armed with a long-range firearm who wreaked havoc on Montreal Monday, leading to the deaths of a police officer, a civilian and ultimately his own, has been identified as a 25-year-old man from Lethbridge, Alta. articles.description: A newly discovered feathered dinosaur called Jian changmaensis may be the missing predator responsible for mysterious piles of crushed prehistoric bird bones in China. articles.title: Scientists open a million-year-old time capsule hidden beneath New Zealand
articles.description: A cave in New Zealand has yielded fossils from a lost ecosystem that existed about 1 million years ago, including a possible flying ancestor of the kākāpō. articles.description: NASA’s upgraded Cold Atom Lab is turning the International Space Station into a frontier for quantum research, creating ultra-cold matter that behaves in astonishing ways. articles.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

articles.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
articles.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

articles.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
articles.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.
